danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles from mutant soldiers and military units
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring evasion or counter-attack

danger_signals.visual_form: Hazardous environmental elements within level design
danger_signals.meaning: Collision-based danger that causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Large enemy entity at stage completion (e.g., Orcus as first boss)
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring defeat to progress; signifies level culmination

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct enemy sprite designs representing General Akhboob's forces

safety_signals.visual_form: Captive characters marked for rescue within levels
safety_signals.meaning: Objective markers; saving them advances mission purpose
Tactical resources that enhance player defensive/offensive capability

safety_signals.visual_form: Captain Carnage and Major Mayhem positioned at level start
safety_signals.meaning: Safe deployment zone before active combat

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Captain Carnage and Major Mayhem; Rambo-inspired soldier aesthetic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Infiltrating Akhboob's 'Baby Milk Factory' base and holding facilities

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mutant soldiers and military units under centralized command
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Fortified military complex; POW detention areas; boss entities like Orcus

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Fictional country of Kookistan under hostile occupation (1999 setting)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Military infrastructure, fortified bases, enemy deployment across scrolling levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mutant soldiers patrol and attack; bosses defend strategic positions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Captive characters visible within levels as interactive rescue objectives
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Players must reach and protect rescued POWs while fighting through enemy waves

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive boss encounters marking stage completion (Orcus in Stage 1)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty increases; larger enemy vehicles and mutant variants appear in later stages

environmental_storytelling.example: Abu Ghraib Infant Formula Plant reference grounds narrative in Persian Gulf War-influenced political scenario
Mutant soldiers visually represent Akhboob's unnatural army, signaling dystopian threat
POWs scattered throughout scrolling levels communicate occupation and captivity; their presence justifies player progression
Multi-stage facility ('Baby Milk Factory') with boss defenders suggests military stronghold narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Fire projectiles at player; organized hostile engagement
Presents heightened threat; requires sustained defeat to unlock level progression
Stationary until player interaction; upon rescue, advance narrative objective
Traverse stages, add environmental hazard dimension to standard combat

notes: Total Carnage uses a dual-character design (Captain Carnage and Major Mayhem) with twin-stick shooter mechanics to communicate faction identity through character pairing. Political context (General Akhboob loosely based on Saddam Hussein; setting influenced by 1991 Persian Gulf War) is conveyed through enemy faction aesthetics and base architecture rather than explicit narrative UI. The scrolling level design contrasts with single-screen arenas, allowing visual communication of expanding military infrastructure and escalating threats. Boss placement at stage conclusions serves dual function: mechanical gate and world-state indicator (dictator's commanders defending territory).
